attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a warm slice of bread on the counter in front of the sink.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene is a kitchen environment with multiple countertops, sinks, and appliances. The relevant objects for the task are identified below:

1. Objects and their types:
   - Receptacles:
     - Microwave: A stainless steel microwave with red control knobs and a digital display showing "OFF". It is mounted above the sink area. It is openable and currently not opened.
     - Sink: A double-basin stainless steel sink with two faucets. It is a receptacle and is not openable.
     - Fridge: Partially visible on the right side of the scene; appears to be a standard refrigerator. It is openable and currently closed.
   - Objects:
     - Knife: A knife with a yellow handle is lying on the countertop to the right of the toaster, near the sink. It is a knife type.
     - Bread: A brown, round loaf of bread is visible on the countertop in the first image, near a wine glass and salt shaker. It is an object.
     - Wine glass, salt shaker, and other small items are present but not relevant to the task.

2. Quantities:
   - 1 microwave
   - 1 double sink (treated as one receptacle for domain purposes)
   - 1 fridge
   - 1 knife
   - 1 loaf of bread

3. Spatial relationships:
   - The microwave is mounted above the sink.
   - The knife is on the countertop to the right of the toaster and in front of the sink.
   - The bread is initially on a separate countertop (left side of the scene) near a trash can and wine glass.
   - The sink is centrally located under the microwave.
   - The fridge is to the right of the sink area.

4. Current state of each object relevant to the planning task:
   - Microwave: Not opened, not on (display shows OFF), not toggled.
   - Sink: Not opened (not applicable as it is not openable), no object is currently in it.
   - Fridge: Closed, not opened.
   - Knife: On the countertop, not held by any agent, not used.
   - Bread: On the countertop, not held, not sliced, not heated, not cooled, not cleaned.

5. Colors and visual properties:
   - Microwave: Stainless steel body with red knobs and a red digital display.
   - Sink: Stainless steel with two basins and two faucets; shiny metallic surface.
   - Knife: Yellow handle, metallic blade.
   - Bread: Brown, round, and appears whole (not sliced).
   - Countertops: Dark gray or brown, with wooden cabinets below.
   - Floor: Tiled with large gray tiles.

The task requires the robot to obtain a slice of bread, heat it, and place it on the counter in front of the sink. The bread must first be sliced using the knife, then heated in the microwave, and finally placed on the counter. The robot must navigate to each object’s location to perform the required actions.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_warm_slice_of_bread)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        microwave1 - microwave
        sink1 - sink
        fridge1 - fridge
        knife1 - knife
        bread1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 microwave1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 fridge1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 bread1))
        (openable microwave1)
        (not (opened microwave1))
        (not (isOn microwave1))
        (not (isToggled microwave1))
        (openable fridge1)
        (not (opened fridge1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 knife1))
        (not (holds agent1 bread1))
        (not (isSliced bread1))
        (not (isHot bread1))
        (not (isCool bread1))
        (not (isClean bread1))
        (atLocation knife1 sink1)  ; knife is on the counter in front of the sink
        (atLocation bread1 sink1)  ; bread is on a countertop, assumed to be near sink area for simplicity
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle bread1 sink1)  ; bread is placed on the counter in front of the sink
        (isHot bread1)
        (isSliced bread1)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_warm_slice_of_bread)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        microwave1 - microwave
        sink1 - sink
        fridge1 - fridge
        knife1 - knife
        bread1 - object
    )
    (:init
        (not (atLocation agent1 microwave1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 fridge1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 bread1))
        (openable microwave1)
        (not (opened microwave1))
        (not (isOn microwave1))
        (not (isToggled microwave1))
        (openable fridge1)
        (not (opened fridge1))
        (not (holdsAny agent1))
        (not (holds agent1 knife1))
        (not (holds agent1 bread1))
        (not (isSliced bread1))
        (not (isHot bread1))
        (not (isCool bread1))
        (not (isClean bread1))
        (atLocation knife1 sink1)  ; knife is on the counter in front of the sink
        (atLocation bread1 sink1)  ; bread is on a countertop, assumed to be near sink area for simplicity
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle bread1 sink1)  ; bread is placed on the counter in front of the sink
        (isHot bread1)
        (isSliced bread1)
    ))
)

        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Put a warm slice of bread on the counter in front of the sink.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene is a kitchen environment with multiple objects and receptacles relevant to the planning task. The robot agent is not initially positioned at any object's location.

**Objects and Types:**

- **Receptacles:**
  - *Microwave*: A stainless steel microwave with a red digital display showing "OFF". It is mounted above a double sink. It is openable and currently closed. Inside, there is a white plate and a red object (possibly a tomato or apple). It is not currently on.
  - *Sink*: A double-basin stainless steel sink with two faucets. It is a receptacle and is not openable. It is currently empty.
  - *Fridge*: Only a small portion of a white fridge is visible on the far right, next to the counter. It is a receptacle and is assumed to be closed.

- **Objects:**
  - *Knife*: A knife with a yellow handle is lying on the countertop between the toaster and the sink. It is an object of type `knife`.
  - *Bread*: Not explicitly visible in any of the images. It is implied to be the object that needs to be sliced, heated, and placed on the counter.
  - *Toaster*: A black, four-slot toaster is on the counter to the left of the sink. It is an object and is not a receptacle.
  - *Apple*: A golden-yellow apple is on a separate counter area with a dark circular sink. This apple is not relevant to the task of preparing bread.
  - *Wine Glass*: A wine glass is on the same counter as the apple. Not relevant.
  - *Salt Shaker*: A white salt shaker is on the counter with the apple. Not relevant.
  - *Trash Can*: A golden trash can is on the floor next to the counter with the apple. Not relevant.

**Quantities:**

- 1 Microwave
- 1 Double Sink
- 1 Fridge (partially visible)
- 1 Knife
- 1 Toaster
- 1 Apple (irrelevant)
- 1 Wine Glass (irrelevant)
- 1 Salt Shaker (irrelevant)
- 1 Trash Can (irrelevant)

**Spatial Relationships:**

- The microwave is directly above the double sink.
- The toaster is to the left of the double sink on the same countertop.
- The knife is on the countertop, positioned between the toaster and the sink.
- The apple, wine glass, and salt shaker are on a separate counter area with a dark sink, which is not the same as the double sink where the task will be completed.
- The trash can is on the floor, adjacent to the counter with the apple.
- The fridge is to the right of the double sink area.

**Current State of Objects (Relevant to Task):**

- The microwave is **closed** and **off**.
- The sink is **empty** and **not openable**.
- The knife is **on the countertop**, not held by the agent.
- The bread is not visible, so its state (sliced, heated, etc.) is unknown and must be acquired and processed by the agent.
- The toaster is **off** and **not in use**.

**Visual Properties:**

- The microwave has a metallic finish with red control knobs and a red digital display.
- The sink is stainless steel with two separate basins and two faucets.
- The knife has a distinct yellow handle and a metallic blade.
- The toaster is black with a matte finish and four slots.
- The counter surfaces are a light brown or beige color.
- The floor is tiled with large, dark gray tiles.
- The apple is shiny and golden-yellow, clearly distinguishable from other objects.

The task requires the robot to find bread (not visible), slice it with the knife, heat it in the microwave, and then place the warm, sliced bread on the counter in front of the sink. The robot must navigate to the relevant objects and perform the necessary actions in sequence.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem put_warm_slice_of_bread)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        sink - sink
        fridge - fridge
        knife - knife
        bread - object
        counter - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
        (not (isHot bread))
        (not (isSliced bread))
        (atLocation robot counter)
        (inReceptacle bread microwave)
        (inReceptacle knife counter)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle bread counter)
        (isHot bread)
        (isSliced bread)
        (atLocation robot counter)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_warm_slice_of_bread)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        sink - sink
        fridge - fridge
        knife - knife
        bread - object
        counter - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (isToggled microwave))
        (not (isHot bread))
        (not (isSliced bread))
        (atLocation robot counter)
        (inReceptacle bread microwave)
        (inReceptacle knife counter)
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle bread counter)
        (isHot bread)
        (isSliced bread)
        (atLocation robot counter)
    ))
)

                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )

```pddl
(define (problem put_warm_bread_slice)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        microwave1 - microwave
        sink1 - sink
        fridge1 - fridge
        counter1 - receptacle  ; counter in front of sink
        counter2 - receptacle  ; counter with apple, salt shaker, etc.
        counter3 - receptacle  ; counter with toaster
        knife_yellow - knife
        knife_black - knife
        bread - object
        apple_gold - object
        salt_shaker - object
        wine_glass - object
        black_rect_object - object
        toaster1 - object
        trash_can - object
    )
    (:init
        ; agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent1 microwave1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 fridge1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 counter1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 counter2))
        (not (atLocation agent1 counter3))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife_yellow))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife_black))
        (not (atLocation agent1 bread))
        (not (atLocation agent1 apple_gold))
        (not (atLocation agent1 salt_shaker))
        (not (atLocation agent1 wine_glass))
        (not (atLocation agent1 black_rect_object))
        (not (atLocation agent1 toaster1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 trash_can))

        ; receptacles properties
        (openable microwave1)
        (not (opened microwave1))
        (openable fridge1)
        ; sink1 is not openable (as per domain, sink is receptacle but not openable)
        ; counters are not openable

        ; objects locations
        (atLocation knife_yellow counter1)
        (atLocation knife_black counter2)
        (atLocation apple_gold counter2)
        (atLocation salt_shaker counter2)
        (atLocation wine_glass counter2)
        (atLocation black_rect_object counter2)
        (atLocation toaster1 counter3)
        (atLocation trash_can counter2)

        ; microwave is off
        (not (isOn microwave1))

        ; bread is not visible, so we assume it must be obtained from toaster or fridge
        ; we'll assume bread is initially in the toaster (common scenario)
        (inReceptacle bread toaster1)

        ; no object is held by agent
        (not (holdsAny agent1))

        ; no object is clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled initially
        (not (isClean bread))
        (not (isHot bread))
        (not (isCool bread))
        (not (isSliced bread))
        (not (isToggled microwave1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        ; bread must be sliced
        (isSliced bread)
        ; bread must be heated
        (isHot bread)
        ; bread must be on the counter in front of the sink (counter1)
        (inReceptacle bread counter1)
    ))
)
```

attempts.problem:
(define (problem put_warm_bread_slice)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent1 - agent
        microwave1 - microwave
        sink1 - sink
        fridge1 - fridge
        counter1 - receptacle  ; counter in front of sink
        counter2 - receptacle  ; counter with apple, salt shaker, etc.
        counter3 - receptacle  ; counter with toaster
        knife_yellow - knife
        knife_black - knife
        bread - object
        apple_gold - object
        salt_shaker - object
        wine_glass - object
        black_rect_object - object
        toaster1 - object
        trash_can - object
    )
    (:init
        ; agent is not at any location initially
        (not (atLocation agent1 microwave1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 sink1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 fridge1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 counter1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 counter2))
        (not (atLocation agent1 counter3))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife_yellow))
        (not (atLocation agent1 knife_black))
        (not (atLocation agent1 bread))
        (not (atLocation agent1 apple_gold))
        (not (atLocation agent1 salt_shaker))
        (not (atLocation agent1 wine_glass))
        (not (atLocation agent1 black_rect_object))
        (not (atLocation agent1 toaster1))
        (not (atLocation agent1 trash_can))

        ; receptacles properties
        (openable microwave1)
        (not (opened microwave1))
        (openable fridge1)
        ; sink1 is not openable (as per domain, sink is receptacle but not openable)
        ; counters are not openable

        ; objects locations
        (atLocation knife_yellow counter1)
        (atLocation knife_black counter2)
        (atLocation apple_gold counter2)
        (atLocation salt_shaker counter2)
        (atLocation wine_glass counter2)
        (atLocation black_rect_object counter2)
        (atLocation toaster1 counter3)
        (atLocation trash_can counter2)

        ; microwave is off
        (not (isOn microwave1))

        ; bread is not visible, so we assume it must be obtained from toaster or fridge
        ; we'll assume bread is initially in the toaster (common scenario)
        (inReceptacle bread toaster1)

        ; no object is held by agent
        (not (holdsAny agent1))

        ; no object is clean, hot, cool, sliced, or toggled initially
        (not (isClean bread))
        (not (isHot bread))
        (not (isCool bread))
        (not (isSliced bread))
        (not (isToggled microwave1))
    )
    (:goal (and
        ; bread must be sliced
        (isSliced bread)
        ; bread must be heated
        (isHot bread)
        ; bread must be on the counter in front of the sink (counter1)
        (inReceptacle bread counter1)
    ))
)

final.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)

```pddl
        ; bread must be sliced
        (isSliced bread)
        ; bread must be heated
        (isHot bread)
        ; bread must be on the counter in front of the sink (counter1)
        (inReceptacle bread counter1)
    ))
)
```
